title: epidendrum coilotrienum
intro: Epidendrum coilotrienum, also known as the 'Fiddle Orchid', is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Epidendrum coilotrienum are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the roots of the Fiddle Orchid are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
